sections.subtitle: Current Form Analysis
sections.text: Bologna shows mixed form in their last 5 matches with 1 win, 2 draws, and 2 losses, but they have been competitive, scoring in 4 of those games. Genoa, on the other hand, is struggling, with no wins in their last 6 matches (3 draws, 3 losses) and conceding in all 5 recent games. This suggests Bologna has the edge in terms of recent performance.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, results have been evenly split with a 2-2 draw and a 2-0 win for Genoa. Both matches indicate tight contests, often with goals from both sides, pointing towards a potentially close game again.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics Overview
sections.text: Bologna ranks higher in the league (8th with 62 points) compared to Genoa (13th with 40 points), showcasing better overall performance. Bologna's stats reflect stronger attacking (56 goals scored) and defensive (44 conceded) numbers than Genoa (34 scored, 48 conceded), alongside superior possession (58.5%) and chance creation.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Tactics
sections.text: Bologna's attacking threat is led by Riccardo Orsolini (14 goals) and Dan Ndoye (8 goals), likely to exploit Genoa's defensive frailties. Genoa relies on Andrea Pinamonti (10 goals) for goals, but their lack of form and missing key players could hinder their output.
